summary: Iteration 2 testing of CoproManager covering new features: Suppliers, Fiscal Years/Budgets/Close-Reopen, Fund Calls (create/get/generate-entries/list/delete), Reports (grand-livre, balance, bilan, resultat, decompte, PDF decompte via reportlab), Coproprietes (auth-gated CRUD + superadmin-only delete), Admin Users CRUD, VCS code generation with mod-97 validation, and Journal entries. Total: 68/68 pytest cases PASS (37 new + 31 regression from iteration 1). Frontend: all 16 sidebar routes load (Coproprietes, Proprietaires, Lots, Locataires, Fournisseurs, Plan Comptable, Exercices, Journaux, Grand Livre, Facturation, Appels de fonds, Banque, Compteurs, Bilan & Resultats, Documents, Utilisateurs) - 0 critical UI errors. Login as admin@copro.be works and role is correctly 'superadmin'. Sidebar correctly groups the 5 sections (Gestion, Comptabilite, Finance, Rapports, Admin).

minor.issue: When passing the digits-only form of the VCS (after stripping '+++' and '/'), the regex search will NEVER match the stored 'vcs_code' because the stored value keeps the separators (e.g. '+++001/0000/12345+++'). Lookup by digit-only string therefore returns []. Only name-based or full-format search works. Fix: also store/index a normalized 'vcs_clean' field, OR compute regex against a $expr/$regexFind on stripped vcs_code.

created_by is set from user.get('_id', '') but get_current_user converts _id to a string already - low risk, currently works but inconsistent with admin.py which uses str(admin['_id']) accessor that would KeyError if _id was popped.

These routers do NOT depend on get_current_user. They are unauthenticated. /api/coproprietes and /api/admin/users are properly gated. Recommend adding Depends(get_current_user) at the router level for production.

action_items: (MINOR) Fix /api/owners/lookup-vcs so digits-only VCS searches actually match (store vcs_clean OR use $regex on a stripped representation).

(SECURITY, recommended) Add Depends(get_current_user) on suppliers, fiscal, fund_calls, reports, properties, accounting routers - currently they accept unauthenticated requests. Coproprietes & admin are correctly gated.
(COSMETIC) Backend log shows seeded 'Test User' fixtures from prior iterations remain in DB - admin users view shows several test_xxx@copro.be entries. Optional one-time cleanup.
critical_code_review_comments: Authentication is inconsistent: only coproprietes & admin routers require auth. All financial endpoints (suppliers, fund-calls, fiscal/budgets, reports) are open to anonymous requests over the public ingress. This is a HIGH-priority issue before any production deployment.
/api/owners/lookup-vcs digit-stripped path is dead code (the regex can never match the stored value with separators).
Routers correctly use empty-string prefixes ('') instead of '/' avoiding the 307 redirect/Mixed-Content issue from iteration 1. Good.

Fiscal close logic computes balances and creates 'AN' a-nouveau journal entry correctly with totals (debit/credit). resultat is computed from class 6 vs class 7. PDF generation via reportlab returns a valid PDF (verified content-type + %PDF magic bytes).
VCS generation uses an atomic counters collection ($inc with upsert) + mod-97 check digit (uses 97 when mod is 0). Verified the mod-97 algorithm is correct for the generated codes.
Fund-call distribution falls back to lot tantiemes when no distribution_key_id is provided - correct.
JWT cookies set with secure=False - acceptable for preview but MUST be True in production behind HTTPS.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (relaxed role assertion to accept admin or superadmin)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (16/16 sidebar routes load, login + role display correct, 0 page errors)

seed_data_creation: All test resources prefixed with TEST2_ for easy cleanup. Tests self-cleanup created suppliers, fiscal years/budgets, owners, fund calls, admin users, and coproprietes in their respective test methods.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend has 2 pytest files: tests/backend_test.py (31 tests - original CRUD) and tests/test_new_features.py (37 tests - iteration 2 features). Run both with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. Open known gaps: (1) /api/owners/lookup-vcs digit-only search is broken-by-design (cosmetic/minor), (2) most routers do not require auth - main agent should add Depends(get_current_user) for production. CODA import was not tested with a real CODA file. PDF generation verified via reportlab.
rca_of_minor_issues: lookup-vcs: vcs_code is stored verbatim as '+++NNN/NNNN/NNNCC+++'. The route computes clean = vcs.replace('+','').replace('/','') and then runs a regex on the original vcs_code field. The regex can never find a contiguous digit substring in '+++001/0000/12345+++'. Fix: either query against a $expr+$regexMatch on a computed stripped value, or add a vcs_digits field at insert time.
